LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT KENTILE FLOORS, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT KENTILE FLOORS, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. DEFAULT SET ASIDE ON GROUND OF ATTORNEY MISTAKE. DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY IS TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF FOR COSTS AND FEES INCURRED IN OBTAINING THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT BUT NOT THOSE INCURRED IN OPPOSING THIS MOTION. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO COST, IF UNABLE TO RESOLVE, PLAINTIFF MAY FILE SEPARATE MOTION TO RECOVER COST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Judge Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, 3689.

Dean A. Hanley, Esq. and Jennifer Maier, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Catherine Serrao, et al.
Dean A. Hanley was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant A.W. Chesterton Company.
Default entered against defendant on May 23, 2008.
Order waiving witness appearance at the default prove-up hearing was filed on August 5, 2008.

Exhibit 1---Five Spreadsheets - One for each the 5 groups of plaintiffs--------------ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiffs, and against the Defaulted Defendant A.W. Chesterton Company as follows:

Interest - 18,420.95.
Attorney's Fees - $2,000.00.
Costs - $371.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $20,791.95.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
